text_blocks.text: The Byzantines were able to regain
control of the country after a brief Sasanian
Persian invasion early in the 7th century
602-688 during which they established a new
short-lived province for ten years known as
was invaded and conquered by the Islamic
Empire by the Muslim Arabs. When they
defeated the Byzantine Armies in Egypt, the
Arabs brought Sunni Islam to the country.
Early in this period, Egyptians began to blend
their new faith with indigenous beliefs
and practices, leading to various Sufi orders
that have flourished to this day. These
earlier rites had survived the period of Coptic
